payload.goal: Make record staleness self-announcing: derive freshness signals at fold time from events already in the log — mechanical events (file_touched/command_run) and task changes appended after the last session_ended, phases still active while every task is done, and last-session summaries clipped by the status block's budget — and surface them on every read surface (SessionStart context block, sofar status, get_state digest) so a resuming session knows WHAT to distrust before it acts on a stale next_action. Zero new event types, zero capture burden: the log already carries the signals; the engine just has to announce them.

tasks.title: Freshness counter in fold: track events appended after the last session_ended (file_touched, command_run, task_status_changed, note_added, decision_logged — any source incl. cli) and expose state.freshness = { events_since_writeback by kind, last_writeback_ts }; zero new event types
Stale-active-phase detection: extract doctor's inline D-P11 check (all tasks done, phase not done) into a shared core derivation; doctor.ts consumes it — one detector, two surfaces

Truncation-aware clip: shared.ts clip/clipBlock variants that report whether they cut — the summary-truncation signal for any budgeted section (budgets live in the template, so detection happens at the render seam)

phases.name: Phase 2 — surfacing on every read surface

tasks.title: Staleness line in renderStatus (new per-section budget const): '⚠ next action may be stale: N events since write-back (files/commands/tasks breakdown)' — rendered only when freshness reports drift; the shared seam covers BOTH the SessionStart block and get_state digest (get-state.ts delegates to renderStatus)
Stale-active-phase marker on phase lines in renderStatus + renderFullStatus: budget-safe suffix like '[active — all tasks done; mark phase done?]', reusing the 1.2 shared detector
renderFullStatus (sofar status): uncapped staleness section — event breakdown since write-back, stale phases, clipped-summary pointer (terminal surface, no 10k cap)
Clipped-summary pointer: when 1.3 reports the last-session summary was cut in renderStatus, append '(clipped — full text in sessions/<id>.md)' inside SESSION_SUMMARY_BUDGET

SPEC §State: document the freshness derivation (events_since_writeback shape, D-P11 detector reuse, zero-new-event-types constraint); §MCP tools + §Hooks: the staleness line in digest and SessionStart block
SPEC §Acceptance: staleness criteria — a stale log renders the warning and a fresh log renders none; 10k SessionStart cap holds; doctor stale-phase parity preserved after extraction

Fold tests: freshness over synthetic logs — events after session_ended counted by kind (incl. source cli), counter resets on a new write-back, replay deterministic (same log → deep-equal state incl. freshness)

Render tests: staleness line present on stale state and absent on fresh; new budget respected; enforceStatusLimit ≤10k with all sections at worst case; clipped-summary pointer fires only when actually clipped
Doctor regression: stale-phase WARN behavior byte-identical after the extraction to core (Phase 11 acceptance still passes)

payload.chose: Derive staleness at fold time from events already in the log and surface it through the existing projections (the shared renderStatus seam already feeds the SessionStart block and the get_state digest; renderFullStatus feeds sofar status)
payload.over: New event types (e.g. staleness_marked), a background watcher/daemon that stamps freshness, or hook-side timestamp bookkeeping
payload.because: The log already carries every needed signal — ulid/ts event order, mechanical events after the last session_ended, and the task-vs-phase divergence doctor D-P11 already detects — and capture burden must stay zero (the record's core discipline): staleness detection that demands new writes would itself go stale, while read-side derivation is compaction-proof and retroactively covers every existing record.

payload.summary: Scaffolded the staleness-detection initiative: goal, 4-phase/12-task plan (fold-time freshness derivation, surfacing via the shared renderStatus seam, SPEC, tests), one framing decision. No code, no SPEC edits.
payload.next_action: User reviews the staleness-detection plan before any build.

payload.chose: Staleness plan approved (founder GO, Jul 11) with a binding review constraint: every staleness flag derives from MECHANICAL signals only — next_action age, mechanical events after last write-back, unclosed sessions, task-vs-phase divergence, render-side clip detection
payload.over: Any inference of staleness from content semantics (e.g. judging whether the next_action text still makes sense against the code)
payload.because: Content-semantic inference is the guessing banned at D3/D12 — a confidently wrong staleness verdict is worse than none; mechanical signals are deterministic, replayable, and free from the log

payload.phase: Phase 2 — surfacing on every read surface
Phase 2 — surfacing on every read surface

payload.chose: Extend SPEC §State/§MCP tools/§Hooks/§CLI with the freshness contract (events_since_writeback by kind + last_writeback_ts, session_ended as the only reset, staleActivePhases as the shared D-P11 detector) and the exact surfacing rules per surface (budgeted line in SessionStart/digest, uncapped Staleness section in sofar status, in-budget clipped-summary pointer)
payload.over: Leaving staleness behavior implementation-defined outside the SPEC, or documenting only the two surfaces the task named (digest + SessionStart) while sofar status changed too
payload.because: docs/SPEC.md is authoritative over improvisation (CLAUDE.md) — the staleness signals are now cross-surface contracts consumed by hooks, MCP and CLI alike, and every surface whose output changed (including sofar status under §CLI) must have its contract updated in the same pass or the SPEC silently drifts from the code it governs

payload.summary: Built staleness-detection end to end, 12/12 tasks: fold-time freshness (events_since_writeback by kind + last_writeback_ts, session_ended sole reset), staleActivePhases extracted from doctor (WARN byte-identical), clipDetect/clipBlockDetect, staleness line + stale-phase markers + clip pointer in renderStatus (SessionStart + digest via shared seam), uncapped Staleness section in sofar status, SPEC extended (State/MCP/Hooks/CLI/Acceptance, decision logged), 22 new tests. Gates green: 353 tests (was 331), typecheck clean. Mechanical signals only per the founder constraint.
payload.next_action: Review the staleness-detection diff on branch staleness-detection (worktree, unpushed) and decide whether to merge and cut a release.

payload.text: Live validation incident (2026-07-11): the 0.5.0 publish ran in session 83ed1f73's tail AFTER its write-back (command_run events 10:10:12–10:13:52Z; note 01KX8APZH9J6EQ116ZDNMMMTNP at 10:14:04Z "published + propagation confirmed"), so next_action stayed "Publish 0.5.0 to npm". The next session 5fb51a69 (status injected at its 10:13:47Z session_started) trusted the stale pointer, and its end_session re-asserted it with a fresh timestamp — staleness laundered through the write-back loop itself. The Phase-2 warning ("next action may be stale: N events since write-back") would have fired here: ≥2 command_run events sat between the 10:10:01Z write-back and the injection. This incident is the concrete evidence for the pending merge decision.

CORRECTION to note 01KX8B4T2G8QC6ZCJDTQAZB16W (it said "pending merge decision" / earlier session called the branch unmerged — wrong): staleness-detection was already merged (812347d) and RELEASED in 0.4.0 (2026-07-11 09:28Z). The morning incident happened anyway because the machine's global install (/Users/jins/.local, hook shims exec it) was still 0.3.2 — deployment lag, not a merge gap. Resolved 2026-07-11: upgraded global to 0.5.0; live fire confirmed (status rendered "⚠ next action may be stale: 5 events since the last write-back — 5 commands" on real drift); 372 tests green on the merged tree. Initiative is shipped end-to-end; this record's own next_action ("review diff, decide merge") is superseded by this note.
